title: luzula sudetica var. frigida
intro: Luzula sudetica var. frigida, also known as a type of woodrush, is a perennial plant species within the family Juncaceae. It is primarily found in cold and mountainous regions, and its name suggests its adaptation to cold temperatures. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of saponins and other compounds. It has been used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and infusions, to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and wounds.
